evidence: AIAgent.__init__ accepts ~40 keyword parameters covering API config, provider routing, callbacks, session management, and feature flags -- a wide implicit parameter bag that grows with every feature addition (run_agent.py:213-257)

tools/__init__.py re-exports 81+ symbols across 264 LOC, duplicating the discovery that model_tools.py + tools.registry already provide; callers in practice import from model_tools, not tools/__init__

gateway/platforms/base.py contains three near-identical cache utility sets (image, audio, document) with the same get_dir/from_bytes/from_url/cleanup pattern copy-pasted, differing only in directory name and content-type defaults

model_tools.py backward-compat wrappers (get_all_tool_names, get_toolset_for_tool, etc.) are pure forwarding to registry but have 5 active callers across cli.py, run_agent.py, doctor.py, banner.py -- functioning as intentional facade, matching the skip criterion
BasePlatformAdapter (gateway/platforms/base.py) has 7 concrete subclasses (Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, Slack, Signal, Email, HomeAssistant) -- well-justified base class with real extension pressure
HermesAgentBaseEnv has 5+ concrete subclasses for different RL environments -- legitimate abstraction hierarchy

ToolRegistry (tools/registry.py) is a clean self-registration pattern: each tool module calls register() at import time, model_tools.py triggers discovery then delegates -- no unnecessary wrapper chains
utils.py is small (89 LOC), tightly scoped to atomic file writes, and genuinely used across modules -- not a generic helper dumping ground

abstraction_fitness.dimension_character: The codebase generally favors direct, domain-aligned abstractions -- the tool registry pattern is clean, base classes have genuine extension pressure with multiple concrete implementations, and utils.py is small and focused rather than a generic dumping ground. However, the AIAgent constructor's ~40-parameter surface acts as an implicit configuration bag that accumulates features without structural boundaries, and the tools/__init__.py barrel export is largely redundant with the registry-based system. Duplicated cache utilities in the platform base show missed opportunities for abstraction where it would actually reduce code.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The codebase is solid but uneven from an abstraction fitness perspective. Core patterns (tool registry, platform adapter hierarchy, environment base class) are well-designed and direct. The score is held back primarily by the AIAgent parameter bag which represents a recurring friction point for any developer constructing or modifying agent instances, and by the redundant tools/__init__.py barrel. These are not severe structural problems but represent enough inconsistency that a developer encounters 'why is this different?' moments. Anchoring at 76: mostly well-served but with recurring friction from the wide constructor and redundant re-export layer.

issues.summary: AIAgent.__init__ accepts ~40 parameters acting as an implicit configuration bag
evidence: AIAgent.__init__ (run_agent.py:213-257) has parameters spanning API config (base_url, api_key, provider, api_mode), provider routing (providers_allowed, providers_ignored, providers_order, provider_sort, provider_require_parameters, provider_data_collection), callbacks (tool_progress_callback, thinking_callback, reasoning_callback, clarify_callback, step_callback), session management (session_id, session_db, honcho_session_key, honcho_manager, honcho_config), and feature flags (save_trajectories, skip_context_files, skip_memory, checkpoints_enabled, pass_session_id)
Each new feature adds another parameter rather than grouping related concerns into config dataclasses
issues.suggestion: Group related parameters into typed config dataclasses: ProviderConfig (providers_allowed/ignored/order/sort/require_parameters/data_collection), CallbackConfig (tool_progress/thinking/reasoning/clarify/step callbacks), SessionConfig (session_id/session_db/honcho_*), FeatureFlags (save_trajectories/skip_context_files/skip_memory/checkpoints_enabled). Constructor becomes AIAgent(model, provider_config, callbacks, session_config, features).

issues.summary: tools/__init__.py re-exports 81+ symbols redundant with registry-based tool discovery
evidence: tools/__init__.py (264 LOC) manually imports and re-exports 81+ symbols from individual tool modules
model_tools.py already provides the public API (get_tool_definitions, handle_function_call) via tools.registry, making the __init__.py barrel largely unused for production imports
The barrel must be manually kept in sync with new tool additions, creating maintenance burden without clear benefit
issues.suggestion: Reduce tools/__init__.py to only re-export the truly public symbols needed by external callers (if any exist beyond model_tools.py). Most callers should import from model_tools or directly from the specific tool module.

issues.summary: Image, audio, and document cache utilities in base.py repeat the same pattern three times
evidence: Each set follows the same structure: mkdir-ensure, uuid-filename, write bytes, return path. Only the directory name, default extension, and content-type header differ
issues.suggestion: Extract a MediaCache class or factory function parameterized by (cache_dir, default_ext, accept_header) that generates all three variants, or a single cache_from_bytes(data, cache_type, ext) function. The document variant adds filename sanitization which can be an optional parameter.

root_causes: Organic feature growth on the AIAgent class without periodic restructuring of the constructor interface
Parallel evolution of tools/__init__.py barrel exports and the newer registry-based system without deprecating the old approach

add.description: Each tool module calls registry.register() at import time; model_tools.py triggers discovery then delegates to the registry for schema retrieval and dispatch. This eliminates the need for a central routing table and keeps tool definitions co-located with their implementations.

BasePlatformAdapter in gateway/platforms/base.py has genuine extension pressure with 7 concrete subclasses (Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, Slack, Signal, Email, HomeAssistant). The abstract methods (connect, disconnect, send, get_chat_info) define a minimal contract; optional methods (send_image, send_voice, edit_message) have sensible fallback defaults.
utils.py contains only atomic file write utilities (JSON and YAML) at 89 LOC. It avoids becoming a generic helper dumping ground, which is a common anti-pattern in Python projects.

The AIAgent.__init__ accumulates parameters from API config, provider routing, callbacks, session management, and feature flags without grouping into config objects. Each new feature adds another keyword argument, making the constructor progressively harder to understand and use correctly.

model_tools.py provides backward-compatible wrapper functions (get_all_tool_names, check_toolset_requirements, etc.) that forward to the registry singleton. These have 5+ active callers across cli.py, run_agent.py, doctor.py and serve as a stable import path -- an intentional facade, not unnecessary indirection.
